$description: Everline v0.1 candidate tokens derived from works/72ppi/everline_p1.svg. These values are drafts, not frozen decisions.

confirmedRules: Component sizes scale in 8 px steps while preserving internal proportions.

gray-800.$description: Observed subdued foreground and border candidate.

gray-850.$description: Batch 2 candidate (added 2026-07-23, backfilled from an existing SVG value). Recessed container shade sitting between canvas (#1A1A1A) and gray-900/surface (#333333), used in the approved batch-2 SVG exclusively for Kanban column bodies so cards read as sitting on top of the column rather than blending into it. Never formalized into the token file when the SVG was drawn, which caused works/html/batch2/ to reuse background-surface for both the column and its cards, flattening the two into one indistinguishable color.

off-white.$description: Observed switch thumb and light foreground.

foreground-disabled.$description: Off-white foreground, combined with opacity.disabled (not baked into the color as 8-digit hex alpha) so the value stays portable to tools like Illustrator that don't reliably support #RRGGBBAA. Updated 2026-07-19 per user feedback: the prior value (gray-800 #444444, solid) had too little contrast against the gray-600 disabled surface, making disabled controls hard to read as buttons at all rather than clearly-but-legibly muted.

background-overlay.$description: Unified surface for floating/overlay panels (Select and Split-button menu, Menu/Context menu, Tooltip, Modal). Added 2026-07-19 to replace 4 previously uncoordinated values (background-subdued, background-surface, a direct gray-800 reference, and Modal's unaliased #222222) that gave visually-similar floating panels inconsistent depth and, worse, borrowed background-subdued's 'disabled' semantic for interactive menu surfaces. Chosen over gray-800 specifically because gray-800 is border-default — reusing it would make every overlay panel's border invisible against its own fill.

foreground-subdued.$description: Batch 2 candidate (2026-07-20). Medium-emphasis text role (table headers, sidebar section labels, task-card meta text). Backfills a semantic token for a role several existing components (e.g. component.tabs.foreground-default) reached directly into color.base.gray-700 for, bypassing the semantic layer contrary to the project's own token-naming convention. Purely additive — does not change any existing component's rendered value.

motion.$description: Candidate (2026-07-19). No component's open/close or hover transition has been visually specified yet; these are placeholder values pending real product validation, added because Split button, Modal and Tooltip already reference 'needs animation' in spec text without any backing token.

duration-base.$description: Open/close transitions: dropdown, menu, tooltip, modal.

duration-slow.$description: Reserved for larger layout shifts; not yet used by any drawn component.

reduced-motion-rule.$value: prefers-reduced-motion: replace all open/close and hover transitions with an instant state change; never delay keyboard interaction on an animation.
reduced-motion-rule.$description: Generalizes the reduced-motion note already written for Split button to apply to every animated component (Modal, Tooltip, Menu, Select, Toast).

layer.$description: Candidate stacking order (2026-07-19) for components that render outside normal document flow. Added because Everline now has 5 distinct overlay-type components (Select/Split-button/Menu panels, Tooltip, Modal+backdrop, Toast) with no defined order for when they coexist (e.g. a toast confirming an action taken inside an open modal). Values are placeholder integers, not CSS z-index requirements; needs product confirmation before treating as fixed.

overlay.$description: Page-level popovers: Select/Combobox panel, Split-button menu, Menu/Context menu.

tooltip.$description: Above overlay panels, since a tooltip can appear on a trigger inside an open menu/select.

modal.$description: Just above its own backdrop. A tooltip rendered inside an open modal must still out-rank the modal locally; this global scale assumes tooltips are re-parented per context rather than fixed to a single number.

toast.$description: Topmost by convention, so transient feedback (e.g. 'deleted') stays visible even while a modal is open or closing. Product should confirm this is the intended behavior for Everline.

icon.$description: Candidate (2026-07-19). Formalizes the stroke-width already used consistently by every hand-drawn icon across the master sheet.

stroke-width.$description: Matches the .icon CSS class already used throughout works/everline-components-master.svg.

source.$value: shadcn/ui (via its bundled lucide-react integration) when the component is composed from shadcn/ui; otherwise the archived docs/lucide-icons-1.24.0 asset pack directly. Both resolve to the same Lucide icon family.
source.$description: User-specified constraint, 2026-07-19. Does not by itself confirm Taylor Kanban's tech stack; applies to whichever product ends up consuming Everline. Per AGENTS.md, third-party icon sets remain prototype/reference only — Everline still owns its own sizing, alignment and state rules layered on top (see size.icon-sm/icon-md and icon.stroke-width).

disabled.$description: Applied via fill-opacity/opacity (or CSS opacity), not baked into a color value. Used with color.semantic.foreground-disabled so disabled text stays legible-but-muted; kept as a separate multiplier rather than an alpha hex color for portability to tools (e.g. Illustrator) with inconsistent #RRGGBBAA support.

backdrop.$description: Batch 1 candidate (2026-07-19). Applied to color.base.canvas (or #000000) via fill-opacity for the modal scrim; not baked into the color value, for the same portability reason as opacity.disabled.

hover-overlay.$description: Batch 1 candidate (2026-07-19). Subtle white overlay for hover backgrounds on dark surfaces where a solid hover color isn't defined yet (e.g. Tabs hover), applied via fill="#ffffff" fill-opacity.

selected-overlay.$description: Batch 2 candidate (2026-07-20). Tinted selected-row background for component.list-item.* (List/Data table/Kanban column), applied to color.semantic.action-primary via fill-opacity. Deliberately a tint, not Select's existing solid action-primary fill — multi-row persistent selection (a table, a list) reads as a stack of buttons if every selected row is solid-filled; this only applies to the 3 new components, Select/Split-button/Menu are unchanged in this pass.

dragging.$description: Batch 2 cross-component decision (2026-07-23). Applied to the element currently being dragged (Card, Task card). Deliberately lighter than opacity.disabled (0.55) — a dragged item is still the active focus of the interaction, it should read as 'lifted', not muted/unavailable like a disabled control. Paired with a border.width-focus / border-focus outline instead of a drop shadow, per Everline's low-decoration principle: shadows read poorly against this dark palette, an outline doesn't.

control-md.$description: Observed standard button and switch height.

tag-md.$description: Candidate compact component height snapped to the confirmed 8 px scale grid.

step.$description: Confirmed component enlargement and reduction step.

button.$description: Observed from the primary button family in works/72ppi/everline_p1.svg.

width-reference.$description: Observed reference width; production width may grow with content.

thumb-inset.$description: Observed optical inset; not a component scale step.

width-reference.$description: Observed source width; scaling changes this baseline in 8 px steps.

scrollbar-width.$description: Observed optical width; verify before implementation.

select.$description: Batch 1 candidate (2026-07-19). Reuses text-input-like trigger and split-button-like menu surface.

search-match-highlight.$description: Combobox variant: color applied to the matched substring in filtered results.

hover-background.$description: Combine with opacity.hover-overlay via fill-opacity, same technique as tabs.hover-background. Changed 2026-07-19 from a solid gray-700 fill because gray-700 is now background-overlay itself (menu's own panel fill) — a solid hover row in the same color would be invisible.

max-width.$description: Candidate wrap width; not yet product-validated.

modal.$description: Batch 1 candidate (2026-07-19). Focus trapping is a behavioral requirement (see spec), not something a static vector token can express.

background.$description: Changed 2026-07-19 from an unaliased #222222 (the only token in this file that skipped the base -> semantic -> component pattern) to the shared background-overlay token.

backdrop-color.$description: Combine with opacity.backdrop via fill-opacity, not a baked alpha hex.

toast.$description: Batch 1 candidate (2026-07-19). Auto-dismiss timing not yet defined.

height.$description: 56 px, one 8 px step above the standard control-md height.

danger-icon-size.$description: Solid-fill circular danger icon, distinct from the bare-stroke icons used by the other toast variants.

list-item.$description: Batch 2 candidate (2026-07-20). Shared row language for List, Data table, and Kanban column's task-card rows only — deliberately NOT retrofitted onto Select/Split-button/Menu's existing selected-item visuals in this pass (see docs/design-system-v0.1-batch2-draft.md for the retrofit-later note).

row-gap.$description: Optical gap between adjoining individually-rounded rows, per 處處是圓; not a component scale step, same treatment as component.switch.thumb-inset.

background-hover.$description: Combine with opacity.hover-overlay via fill-opacity, same technique as tabs/menu hover.

background-selected.$description: Combine with opacity.selected-overlay via fill-opacity — a tint, not a solid fill.

indicator-width.$description: Left accent bar on selected rows; a decorative accent, distinct from border.width-*.

sidebar.$description: Batch 2 candidate (2026-07-20). Single component with expanded and collapsed (rail) modes.

width-collapsed.$description: Candidate rail width; unverified. Also the minimum width when resizing — dragging narrower than the collapsed rail doesn't make sense, at that point it should just collapse.

width-max.$description: Batch 2 cross-component decision (2026-07-23). Resize ceiling; not visually validated, picked as a reasonable cap so the sidebar can't crowd out the main content area. No snapping/presets in this pass.

notification-dot-color.$description: Candidate; whether notification dots should carry primary or danger semantics is flagged open, not decided in this pass.

card.$description: Batch 2 candidate (2026-07-20). Generic content card, distinct from component.task-card.

border-error.$description: Reuses Inline alert's 4px indicator-bar language rather than inventing a new error treatment.

data-table.$description: Batch 2 candidate (2026-07-20). Single row-height only in this pass; density and pagination are intentionally not yet defined.

kanban-column.$description: Batch 2 candidate (2026-07-20). Must render as individually-rounded cards with gaps — never the flat/table layout referenced in works/TaylorConceptMockup1.svg, which is a structural reference only, not a style source.

width.$description: Corrected 2026-07-23 to match the value actually drawn and user-approved in the 2026-07-20 SVG review (pixel-measured from exports/everline-components-batch2.png); the prior 320px was an unverified candidate that was never what got reviewed. 288 still satisfies the 8px scale grid (288 = 36 x 8).

background.$description: Added 2026-07-23. Column body fill, deliberately darker than component.card/task-card's background-surface so Task cards visually separate from the column instead of blending into it — backfilled from the approved SVG, which used this shade but never had it captured as a token (see color.base.gray-850).

task-card.$description: Batch 2 candidate (2026-07-20). Extends component.card.*; status-dot colors constrained to the existing approved palette, no unapproved success/warning hues.

status-dot-done.$description: No approved success/green token yet; reuses action-primary blue to mean 'complete' pending a real success-color decision.

assignee-avatar-size.$description: Lightweight ad-hoc avatar placeholder for the assignee candidate field — deliberately not component.avatar.*; Avatar/Avatar group remains a second-phase candidate, out of v0.1 scope (tracked in GitHub Issues, not this file).

family-sans.$description: Confirmed UI font stack (user-confirmed 2026-07-19). The source SVG had converted text to outlines, so this could not be verified from the file alone; no longer a candidate value.
